diagrams.description: This timeline traces key milestones of Modern History from the Renaissance to the early 21st century. Major dates include the Fall of Constantinople (1453) and Columbus’s voyage (1492); the Protestant Reformation (1517); the English Civil War (1642‑1651); the American and French Revolutions (1776, 1789); the Industrial Revolution in Britain (c. 1780); the Congress of Vienna (1815); the Year of Revolutions (1848); the unification of Germany and Italy (1861‑1871); the Scramble for Africa (1880s); World War I (1914‑1918); the Russian Revolution (1917); the Great Depression (1929); World War II (1939‑1945); the founding of the United Nations (1945); decolonisation and India’s independence (1947); the Chinese Revolution (1949); the fall of the Berlin Wall (1989) and end of Cold War; 9/11 (2001); and the global financial crisis (2008).
diagrams.mermaid_code:
timeline
    title Key Milestones of Modern History
    section Early Modern Transitions
      1453 : Fall of Constantinople
      1492 : Columbus reaches Americas
      1517 : Protestant Reformation begins
      1642‑1651 : English Civil War
    section Age of Revolutions
      1776 : American Declaration of Independence
      1789 : French Revolution
      c. 1780 : Industrial Revolution in Britain
      1815 : Congress of Vienna
    section Long 19th Century
      1848 : Year of Revolutions
      1861‑1871 : Unification of Italy & Germany
      1880s : Scramble for Africa
    section World Wars & Ideological Conflict
      1914‑1918 : World War I
      1917 : Russian Revolution
      1929 : Great Depression
      1939‑1945 : World War II
      1945 : United Nations founded
    section Decolonisation & Cold War
      1947 : Independence of India & Pakistan
      1949 : Chinese Communist Revolution
      1955‑1975 : Vietnam War
      1989 : Fall of Berlin Wall
    section Contemporary Era
      2001 : 9/11 attacks
      2008 : Global financial crisis
      2020 : COVID‑19 pandemic
